Active Work Architecture
─[ GitHub Multi-Account Push ]─ ┐

name: gh-multi-account description: Switch GitHub accounts when push fails on multi-account machines user-invocable: false allowed-tools: Bash

GitHub Multi-Account Push

Context

When git push fails with permission denied on a machine with multiple authenticated GitHub accounts, the wrong gh account is likely active.

Snags

  • Permission error format: remote: Permission to <owner>/<repo>.git denied to <wrong-user>
  • The active account applies to ALL git operations over HTTPS, not just gh commands.
  • gh auth status shows all accounts — you must look for Active account: true.

Workaround

gh auth status          # see which account is active
gh auth switch --user <correct-user>  # switch to repo owner
git push                # retry

Gotchas

  • Match <owner> in the error to the correct gh account name.
  • After switching, the token persists in the keyring — no re-auth needed.
  • Check docs/knowledge/environment/ for repo-to-account mappings if multiple repos use different accounts.
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
